provenance: Authored slop→clean pair (#58, P0). original.md is a corporate adjective pile ('robust, scalable, and innovative', 'best-in-class', 'leverages', 'seamless, cutting-edge solutions'). clean.md is a STRICT de-claim: it keeps only claim atoms already present in the original ('empowers teams', 'delivers solutions', 'across the enterprise') and drops every superlative/adjective-pile token. It introduces NO new claim — an earlier draft that invented 'distributed workloads'/'integrates with tools' was a lateral swap and was rejected in the #58 Step-11 review.

seeded_defects.note: Adjective pile + laundered superlatives ('best-in-class', 'cutting-edge'). Genre=marketing → recommendation strip. clean.md keeps ONLY claim atoms already in original.md (empower teams / deliver solutions / enterprise) and drops the superlatives, so it introduces no new claim atoms and would pass Layer-1 no_new_claim_atoms. This is the rule-#3 de-claim target: strip the corporate voice hard while staying verifier-safe, without inventing a new capability.
